Kamuya düşen, vatandaşın sağlıklı beslenmesini ve gıdaya erişimini güven altına almaktır, bunun için de kurumlara ve kurallara ihtiyaç vardır. İktidar o kurumları yıkmıştır, biz
kurumları da kuralları da ayağa kaldırmaya kararlıyız.
Çiftçilere hakkını vermeyen iktidar çocuklara da acımamaktadır. Ülkemizde yaklaşık 10 milyon çocuk yoksulluk içinde, 2 milyon çocuk derin yoksulluk içindedir. Bir et çeşidini günde bir kez tüketilebilen çocukların oranı sadece yüzde 12'dir. Türkiye'de 3 öğrenciden 1'i kahvaltı yapmadan okula gitmekte, 5 öğrenciden 1'i okulda hiçbir şey yiyememektedir. Öğrencilere ücretsiz bir öğün yemek verilmesi teklifimizi seçimlerde sahiplenip seçim sonrası vazgeçmiştiniz ve burada Cumhur İttifakı milletvekilleri olarak bu konuda verdiğimiz kanun teklifini de sizler reddettiniz. Her yandaşa kaynak bulan iktidar nedense çocuklarımızın karnını doyurmak için kaynak bulamamaktadır. Cumhuriyet Halk Partisinin, Türkiye ittifakının ilk bütçesinde o kaynağı bulduğumuzu da çocuklarımızı doyurduğumuzu da sizler gözlerinizle göreceksiniz. (CHP sıralarından alkışlar)
Okul kantinlerindeki fiyatlar çocuklarımızın beslenme sorununu gerçek gündemlerimizden biri olarak önümüze koymaktadır. İstanbul Yenibosna'da pazar yerinde çalışan çocuklar "Harçlık için çalışıyorum." dediler bana. Simit fiyatı kantinde 20 lira, küçük su 10 lira, çay 20 lira, tost 50 lira; öğrenci su alsa aç kalıyor, simit alsa susuz kalıyor. Hele hele bir öğrenci sabahleyin bir çay, bir simit alsa öğlen de o tostu değil ayranla, kolayla, suyla katık etse toplam verdiği para 100 liradır, ayda 2.500 liradır. Sayın Erdoğan'ı dinleyip 3 çocuk yapan babanın çocuğuna vereceği, su ve tost yiyen
çocuğuna vereceği toplam harçlık 7.500 liradır. Bugün kantinlerde veresiye defteri vardır. İktidarımızda günde 3 kap sıcak yemeği öğrencilerimize verecek bir bütçeyi bu Meclisten geçireceğiz.
Sayın Cumhurbaşkanı Yardımcısı, eğer buradan bir talimatınız olursa Cumhuriyet Halk Partili belediyeler bölgelerindeki hatta yakınlarındaki tüm okullarda öğrencilere sıcak yemek vermek ve ücretsiz, sağlıklı su vermek için göreve hazırdır. (CHP sıralarından alkışlar) Sadece buradan bir talimatınızı bekliyoruz. Bunu yapmak isteyen belediyelerimizin okul kapılarında engellendiğini de milletimize şikâyet etmek isterim.
Şimdi, çocuklara gelecek kuramayan iktidar, gençlere de umut verememektedir. Tüm dünyada "NEET" kavramıyla ifade edilen ev gençleri vardır. Bu gençler ne eğitimde ne istihdamdadır. Buna Avrupa çok dertlenmektedir, Almanya bu sorunla çalkalanmaktadır. Almanya'daki ne işte ne eğitimde olan çocuk oranı, genç oranı yüzde 6'dır. Avrupa'nın kara kara tasalandığı oran yüzde 9'dur. Türkiye'de bu oran yüzde 25'tir yani her 4 gençten 1'i ne eğitimdedir ne istihdamdadır. Bugün her 4 gençten 3'ü "İmkânım olsa yurt dışında yaşamak isterim." demektedir; deyim yerindeyse gençlerimizin yüzde 75'i valizleri kafada toplamış durumdadır.
Meclisimizde bazı sayın genel başkanlarımız farklı beka sorunları tarif etmektedir ve şöyle demektedirler: "Dünyanın gelişmiş ülkeleri üzerimizde hesap yapıyorlar, hayal kuruyorlar." Kursunlar, bu
beka sorununu yüz yıl önce nasıl bertaraf ettiysek onu bertaraf etmek, bu ülkenin toprakları üzerinde hesap yapanlara haddini bildirmek boynumuzun borcudur. Ama esas beka sorunu gelişmiş ülkelerin bu topraklarda hayal kurması değil bu ülkenin evlatlarının dörtte 3'ünün gelişmiş ülkelerde hayal kurmasıdır. İktidarımızda bu ülkenin her görüşten gençlerine Anadolu'da ve Trakya'da hayal kurdurmayı taahhüt ediyoruz, söz veriyoruz. (CHP sıralarından alkışlar)
Öyle bir adaletsiz düzenin içindeyiz ki artık toplumun canına tak etmiştir, vergide adaletsizlik bunun doruk noktasıdır. 2025 bütçesi olarak getirdiğiniz bütçede dolaylı vergilerin oranı yüzde 65'tir. Dolaylı vergiler dünyanın en adaletsiz vergisidir, zengin-fakir ayırt etmeyen vergi türüdür; fabrikatör ile o fabrikada çalışan işçinin, bekçinin mandıraya gidip peynire aynı vergiyi vermesidir; Türkiye'nin en pahalı cipine binen ile otuz yıllık yorgun bir traktörü sürenin mazota aynı vergiyi vermesidir. Bu oran yüzde 65'tir. Vergilerin yüzde 20'si de maaş alanların eline bile değmeden kesinti yoluyla kesilmektedir. Gerçekten para kazananlardan alınması planlanan vergi bu bütçede yüzde 14,5'tur, gerçekleşmelerin ise yüzde 11'lerde olduğunu geçen seneden tecrübe etmiş durumdayız. Türkiye'nin kaynaklarını emen Hükûmetin övündüğü projelerin müteahhidi 44 firmanın 37'si geçen sene hiç vergi vermemiştir, matrahsız beyannameler bu 37 şirkete aittir. Kırk haramiler
elini cebine atmamakta ama devlet elini çalışanların, mavi, beyaz, gri yakalıların ceplerinden çıkarmamaktadır. Bu vergi düzeni yoksulu ezen, zengini kollayan bir düzendir ve yine, bu bütçede 701 milyar liralık kurumlar vergisi istisnası getirilmektedir. "Kaynak, kaynak, kaynak" diyorsunuz, sadece beşte 1'i etkin teşvike giden, beşte 4'ü birilerinin cebinde kalan yerde aradığınız kaynağı bulabilirsiniz. Biz yaptığımız bütçelerde yapacağımız etki analizleriyle doğru teşvikleri devam ettirip kalkınacağız, yanlış yapılanları ise doğru olanlara kaydırıp hep birlikte büyüyecek ve zenginleşeceğiz.
Konuşmamın başında cumhuriyetin ilk bütçesine atıf yapıp 1924'te Türkiye Cumhuriyeti'nin ilk bütçesinin görüşmelerini hatırlatmıştım. Maliye Vekili Abdülhalik Bey kürsüden şu açıklamayı yapmıştı: "Halkı ezen aşar vergisini hızla kaldıracağız." Daha bir yıl olmadan sözlerini tuttular, üreticinin ürettiğinden onda 1'ini alan aşar vergisini kaldırdılar. Bugün aşar vergisini kaldırmış bu Meclisin 100'üncü yılında öyle adaletsiz bir vergi sistemi vardır ki bugün 12 maaş alan beyaz yakalıların 3 ila 4 maaş kadar vergi ödediklerini biliyoruz. 33 bin lira maaş alan bir çalışan yıl boyunca 3 net maaşını vergi olarak vermektedir, 66 bin lira maaş alan yani yoksulluk sınırında maaş alan bir beyaz yakalı 4 maaşını vergiye veriyor. Eskiden beyaz yakalılar "Maaşım bu kadar ama 16 maaş alacağım." derken bugün 12 maaş alan beyaz yakalı maaşının 4 tanesini de vergiye vermektedir. Bu Meclis aşarı
kaldırmıştır ama çalışanlar Erdoğan'ın aşırı vergisine muhataptırlar. Eskiden 16 maaş alanlar 8 maaşa çalışmakta, 4 maaşlarını Erdoğan'ın aşar vergisine ödemektedirler; bu vergiye isyan ediyoruz. (CHP sıralarından alkışlar)
Diğer yandan da "kaynak" dediğimizde kaynak bulamayanlar -kanunda görev- kanunun çıktığı günden beri, tam on sekiz yıldır, 2006'dan beri Sayın Erdoğan'da, bir dönem Sayın Binali Yıldırım'da, son yedi yıldır yine Sayın Erdoğan'da olduğu hâlde vergi cennetlerinin listesini bir türlü yayınlamamaktadır. Oysa kanun açıktır, vergi cennetleri bellidir; yarın sabah eğer Resmî Gazete'de vergi cennetleri yayınlanırsa o cennetlere giden gelen paradan yüzde 30 vergi alınacaktır. Bermuda'ya, Lüksemburg'a, Virjin Adaları'na, Man Adası'na, Cayman'lara, Malta'ya, Seyşeller'e kim para gönderiyorsa on sekiz yıldır kayırdığınız odur. Siz yapmıyorsunuz; iktidarımızın ilk üç kararnamesinde, bakanları atadıktan, üst düzey devlet yetkililerini atadıktan sonra çıkacak ilk kararnamede bu listeyi yayınlayacağız, sizinkileri vergiye bağlayacağız. (CHP sıralarından alkışlar)
İktidar hem alacağı vergiyi almıyor hem de yoksullardan adaletsizce topladığı vergiyi birilerinin cebine aktarıyor. "Vatandaşın cebinden bir kuruş çıkmayacak." dediğiniz kamu-özel iş birliklerine, köprülere, otoyollara, tünellere maalesef bu bütçede 204 milyar lira yatırdınız. "Bunların hiçbirine bir kuruş çıkmıyor." diyenlerin, onların yerinde
yeller esiyor. Yerine oturanlar, 204 milyar lira bu yıl için, üç yıl için 678 milyar lirayı ayırmışlardır.
Biz Cumhuriyet Halk Partisi olarak asla bu yatırımlara karşı değiliz, âlâsını yapacağız, daniskasını yapacağız ama bunu birilerini zengin etmek için bu modelle yapmayacağız. Siz değil misiniz 10 bin liralık emekli maaşını 12.500'e getirirken öfleyen pöfleyen? 33 milyar lirayı zor bulanlar, vergi ödemeyen yandaşlarına 204 milyar lira kaynak ayırmışlar. Bir diğer yandan da bunlara ödeme yaparken dolar bazında, euro bazında, kendi ülkelerinin enflasyonu da etkilenerek bir seferde... Ama Çayırhan'daki termik santrali yirmi yıl birileri işletmiş, dünyanın parasını kazanmış, bir mucize olmuş, kesilen, altın yumurtlayan tavuk dirilmiş, bizim kümese geri gelmiş "Bir daha keselim." diyorsunuz. Onu verdiğiniz kişiye kömürü veriyorsunuz, onu verdiğiniz kişiye santrali veriyorsunuz, alım garantisini veriyorsunuz "Parayı öderken TL öde, 6 taksitte öde." diyorsunuz. Utanmasaydınız, bir de üstüne kırmızı fiyonk yapsaydınız. Kime veriyorsunuz Çayırhan'ı, kimin malını kime veriyorsunuz? (CHP sıralarından alkışlar)
Bu yüzden şu kadarını söyleyeyim ki: İktidarımızın ilk gününde bu projelerde hemen Türk lirasına döneceğiz, hukuk gözetilerek buraya kamu yararı gözüyle yeniden bakacağız -bu kısmı Selin Hanım yazdı, eğitimli hanımefendi, utanmış, diyememiş- kamulaştıracağız, kamulaştıracağız, kamulaştıracağız! (CHP sıralarından alkışlar)
Ekonomide durum kötüyken yönetimden ne beklenir? Elbette tasarrufa kendisinden başlaması. Yaptınız bir tasarruf genelgesi; genelgeye göre araçlar sınırlanacak, pek çok sınırlama gelecekti. Bir örneğe değineyim: Sayın Cumhurbaşkanı Yardımcım, 120 bin araç için yazı yazdınız "İhtiyaç fazlalarını belirleyin, kiralamayın, geri kalanı da satın." diye. Belirlenen ihtiyaç fazlası bin araç arkadaşlar, bin araç. 120 bin araçtan 119 bini lazım, bini ihtiyaç fazlası ve bu bin araç daha elden çıkarılmadan bu bütçeye 3.544 yeni araç satın alımı konulmuş durumdadır. Yani, sizler o araçlarla, o uçaklarla, o şatafatlarla devam eder ve tasarruf etmezken Devlet Su İşlerinden emekli Mithat amca, bankadan emekli Emel teyze, asgari ücretli kardeşim Muammer, babadan kalan maaşla idare etmeye taşıyan Leyla kardeşim boş buzdolabına bakacak, akşam pazarında ezik ürünler toplayacak. Böyle adalet olmaz! İktidar, tasarrufu yoksuldan ve işçilerden yapıyor. 2.500 engelli öğretmeni atamıyorlar "Tasarruf ettik." diye övünüyorlar. Zaten iktidarın derdi halka refah sağlamak da değil, öyle olsaydı Varlık Fonuna kamu bankalarını, sigorta şirketlerini, limanları, Türk Hava Yollarını devredip, sonra Cumhurbaşkanı Erdoğan Varlık Fonunun başına kendisini atayıp, ondan sonra da Varlık Fonunu Sayıştay denetiminden çıkarmazdınız. Biz geldiğimizde, Sayıştay, Varlık Fonu başta olmak üzere denetimden kaçırılan tüm kurumları denetleyecektir.
Ekonomik kriz derinleşirken iktidar ülkeyi herkes için güvensiz
hâle getirdi. Bugün çocuklarımız güvende değil. Yapılan araştırmalarda toplumun yüzde 86'sı çocukların güvende olmadığını söylüyor. Henüz 8'inde bir kız çocuğu Narin, kaybolduktan on dokuz gün sonra derede bir çuval içinde taşların altında bulundu. "Diclenin kenarında bir koyunu kurt kapsa sorumlusu benim." diyen bir kamu yönetim anlayışından geliyoruz; Hazreti Ömer'den vasiyettir. 8 yaşında bir kız çocuğunu koruyamadık, katillerini tespit edemedik ama bütün köyün bildiğini, "O köyde dostlarımız var, konuşamam." diyen milletvekilinizin bildiğini savcılar bilemiyor, bulamıyor. Araştırmalara göre iki buçuk yılda 64 çocuk Narin gibi yaşamını yitirmiştir.
Bugün çocukların ve gençlerin karşı karşıya kaldığı bir diğer büyük tehdit ise hepinizin bildiği gibi uyuşturucudur. Emniyetin resmî raporlarına göre 2023'te ele geçirilen "kristal" denilen metamfetamin miktarı 2019'a oranla yüzde 2 bin artmıştır. Uyuşturucu ne yazık ki okul önlerine kadar inmiştir. Anneler-babalar, iktidar olsun, muhalefet olsun gördüğü her milletvekiline, ulaştığı her siyasetçiye "Evlatlarımızı bu beladan kurtarın, uzak tutun." diye feryat etmektedir. Zaman zaman birbirini çekemeyen, Sayın Erdoğan sevgisini paylaşamayan halef-selef iki Bakanı dört yılda yüzde 2 bin artan uyuşturucu miktarının sorumluluğunu paylaşmaya davet ediyorum.(CHP sıralarından alkışlar) Uyuşturucu kullanımındaki bu artışın sebepleri araştırılmalıdır ama yapılan araştırmaların pek çoğunda alkoldeki astronomik ve adı konmamış
düşmanca uygulanan yaşam tarzı vergisinin yüksekliğinin etkili olduğu tespitlerine de dikkat çekmek istiyorum.
Çocuklar gibi kadınlar da güvende değildir. OECD'ye göre Avrupa'da kadına karşı şiddette maalesef 1'inci sıradayız. Neredeyse 4 kadından 1'i şiddete maruz kalıyor. Kadınların yüzde 70'i geceleyin sokaklarda güvende yürüyemediklerini söylüyorlar.
Bu iktidar emeklilere, emekçilere, bu iktidar madencilere, bu iktidar esnafa, çiftçiye verdiği sözleri tutamamıştır. Seçime iki gün kala depremzedelere "Bir yılda 650 bin konut yapacağız. Bir yıl sonunda hepiniz evlerinize kavuşacaksınız." deyip iki yıl dolarken sadece yüzde 25'i, mesele Hatay'sa sadece yüzde 12'si... 4 depremzededen 3'ü koyteynerde, 4 depremzededen 3'ü çadırda ya da gurbettedir Türkiye'de. Hatay'da ise evine kavuşanların oranı sadece yüzde 12'dir. Hiçbir söz tutulmamıştır ama bir tek söz tutulmuş, tutulmaya devam edilmiştir; sadece HÜDA PAR'a verdiğiniz İstanbul Sözleşmesi'ne dönmeme sözü ısrarla tutulmaktadır. Cumhuriyet Halk Partisinin ilk iktidar haftasında İstanbul Sözleşmesi Türkiye Büyük Millet Meclisinde yeniden kanunlaşacaktır. (CHP sıralarından alkışlar)
Bu ülkede bebekler de güvende değil. En az 12 yenidoğan yavrumuz ihmalin, denetimsizliğin, para için gözü dönmüşlüğün kurbanı oldu. On dokuz ay önce bilinen ama bebekler ölmeye devam ederken tedbir alınmayan bir ihmalden bahsediyoruz. Bugün bir kamu görevlisinin dahi ifadesi alınmıyor, mahkemelerde âdeta bir tiyatro oynanıyor.
Sistem biliyor ki: "Sarı öküzü verirsem ucu bize kadar geliyor." Bugün yenidoğanları öldüren, bilmediğimiz yenidoğanların ölümüne sebep olmuş olan ya da herhangi birimizin yakınlarını, büyüklerini yoğun bakımlarda telef eden sistem, bu iktidarın sağlığı ticarileştiren, denetimsizleştiren sistemidir. Bu sistemde özel hastaneler ruhsatlar almakta, bonservisler gibi ruhsatlar almakta. İnanabiliyor musunuz, sadece yenidoğan ünitesini istediği birine kiralamakta, o kirayı çıkarmak isteyenler çeteleşmekte, kendi aralarında paslaşmakta, devleti soymakta, bebeleri öldürmektedirler. Sağlığı ticarileştiren bu vicdansızlığın, bu denetimsizliğin hepinizin yüreğini yaktığını biliyorum. Susuyorsunuz, susuyorsunuz ama hepimiz gerçek sorumluları biliyoruz; sarı öküzü bırakın, vicdanınıza sarılın. (CHP sıralarından alkışlar)
Dış politika, üzerinde ciddiyetle durmamız gereken, Türkiye'nin ana meselelerinden bir tanesi. Ülkemizin ve partimizin kurucusu Gazi Mustafa Kemal Atatürk'ün tüm dünyada bilinen "Yurtta barış, dünyada barış." sözünü hatırlamanın tam da zamanı. Atatürk'ün dış politikada bize nasıl bir miras bıraktığını biliyoruz; "Yurtta barış, cihanda barış." vizyonunu bıraktı, komşularla iyi ilişkiler bıraktı. Devam eden yıllarda hem Cumhuriyet Halk Partisi hükûmetleri ve diğer pek çok hükûmet bu vizyona uygun hareket etti; komşuların iç tartışmalarından uzak durdu, komşudaki devlet dışı unsurlarla muhatap olmadı, komşunun toprak bütünlüğünü savundu. Ancak AK PARTİ iktidarları bunun tam tersini yaptı;
dış politikada kurumlar dışlandı, bu Parlamento dışlandı, en önemlisi, Dışişleri Bakanlığı, kadrolarıyla, birikimiyle ve geleneğiyle dışlandı. Kıbrıs Barış Harekâtı sırasında dönemin Başbakan Yardımcısı Erbakan 18 Temmuz günü, dönemin Başbakanı Bülent Ecevit 20 Temmuz günü harekât hakkında ilk bilgilendirmeyi kapalı oturumla bu salonda yaptılar. Oysa Suriye'de olan bu kadar olay karşısında Parlamento tamamen görmezden gelindi.
Suriye'deki maceracı yaklaşım 2011 yılından itibaren vatandaşlarımızın canına kasteden büyük bir güvenlik tehdidini yarattı ve bizi büyük bir göç sorunuyla karşı karşıya bıraktı. Türkiye'nin ödediği maliyeti görmezden gelemeyiz; on üç yılda hiçbir şey olmamış gibi, tüm hataların bedelini halkımız ödememiş gibi, Aylan bebekler ölmemiş, Ege'de boğulmamış gibi hareket edemeyiz. Öncelikle geçmişteki hatalardan ders almalıyız, maceracı dış politika yaklaşımından hızla uzaklaşmalıyız.
Komşumuz Suriye'yi senelerdir otoriterlikle yöneten Esad dün devrildi tıpkı Irak'ta, tıpkı Libya'da olduğu gibi. Atatürk'ün bir tek adam rejimi değil, otoriter bir rejim değil de bizlere demokratik bir cumhuriyet bırakmasının, her ne kadar yıpratılsa, aşındırılsa da ayakta olan kurumları ve kurallarıyla bir demokrasiyi bize emanet etmiş olmasının önemini bir kez daha hatırlamakta fayda var. Yanı başımızda bir ülke paramparça hâle geldi, her parça bir başka küresel gücün elinde oyuncak olma riskiyle karşı karşıya. Şimdi, artık Suriye'de daha fazla kan dökülmeden, iç savaşı kesin bir şekilde
sonlandırmanın, tüm Suriyelileri temsil eden bir geçiş hükûmeti kurmanın zamanıdır. Suriye'de şimdi yaraları sarmanın, demokrasiyi inşa etmenin, insanca bir rejim kurmanın zamanıdır. Biz, Suriye halkı için iyi olanın yanındayız. Biz, Suriye'nin komşularını, bölgedeki uluslararası aktörleri iç savaşın bitirilmesine katkı vermeye, insan haklarına ve hukukun üstünlüğüne dayalı tüm Suriye'yi temsil edecek demokratik bir rejimin kurulmasına yardımcı olmaya çağırıyoruz.
Biz Türkiye'de ana muhalefet partisiyiz, yurt dışına çıktığımızda Türkiye'nin partisiyiz. Temsil edildiğimiz tüm uluslararası kuruluşlarda, kurumlarda Türkiye'nin, Kuzey Kıbrıs Türk Cumhuriyeti'nin, Azerbaycan'ın haklarını savunduk, savunmaya devam ediyoruz. Biz doğruya "doğru" yanlışa "yanlış" diyen bir noktadayız. Örneğin, Rusya-Ukrayna ilişkilerinde taraf olunmayıp denge politikası izlenmesini doğru bulduğumuzu her platformda ifade ettik. Suriye'yle ilgili dünden beri yapılan açıklamalarda Suriye'nin toprak bütünlüğüne yapılan vurgu, demokratik ve özgür seçimler noktasındaki sağduyulu açıklamaları dikkatle takip ediyoruz. Bizim de iktidara çağrımızdır: Türkiye, Suriye'ye maceracılıktan uzak, fatih heveslerinden uzak bir pozisyondan, barışçıl bir pencereden bakmalıdır. Türkiye'nin Suriye politikası siyasi propagandaların malzemesi olmayacak kadar önemlidir. Trollerin akıl dışı heyecanları olabilir, sözde yorumcular, sözde uzmanlar, sırtında yumurta küfesi taşımayanlar macera peşinde koşabilirler ancak devlet ciddi olmak zorundadır, soğukkanlı olmak zorundadır. Suriye'de demokratik, kapsayıcı, hukukun üstünlüğüne dayalı bir rejim inşasından yana olmayan kesimlerden uzak durulmalı, terörün ve şiddetin son bulması için çözümler üretilmelidir. Ülkemizdeki Suriyelilerin
evlerine dönmelerine yardımcı olacak kapsamlı bir geri dönüş programı hazırlanmalıdır. Bugün ülkemizdeki sığınmacılar meydanlarda sevinç gösterilerinde bulunuyorlar. Bu sevinç dönüş sevinci ise buna iştirak ediyoruz ancak iktidarın bir an önce yanıtlaması gereken, meydanlarımızda gösteri yapan bu kadar sığınmacının nasıl gönderileceği sorusudur.
Bizim Suriye'ye dair önceliğimiz, oradaki askerlerimizin güvenliği, Türkiye'nin ve yurttaşlarımızın güvenliği, Türkiye'nin çıkarları ve huzurudur; ne kimsenin maşası olmayı kabul ederiz ne de başka memleketlerde yangına maşayla müdahaleyi doğru buluruz. Cumhuriyet Halk Partisi Suriye'nin toprak bütünlüğünden yanadır, demokrasi, barış ve istikrar ortamından yanadır, Suriye halkından ve iradesinden yanadır. Aksi hâlde yanı başımızda istikrarsızlık üretecek Afganistan gibi bir yapıyla yaşama riskimiz ortadadır. Böyle bir ülkeye Türkiye'deki Suriyeliler de dönmeyecektir, aksine Türkiye yeni göç dalgalarına da maruz kalabilecektir.
Buradan Türkiye'nin 1'inci partisinin Genel Başkanı olarak Avrupa'ya da sesleniyorum: Cumhuriyet Halk Partisi ilk seçimlerde iktidar olacaktır ve Cumhuriyet Halk Partisi yalnızca sınır komşularıyla değil, Avrupa'yla da iyi ilişkiler içinde olmayı istemektedir. Cumhuriyet Halk Partisi, yurttaşların esenliğini ve güvenliğini sizden gelecek hiçbir teklife değişmeyecektir, hakkaniyetli olmayan hiçbir pazarlığa oturmayacaktır. İktidarımızda, Türkiye, komşularının yanında duracak, hem de Avrupa sisteminin parçası olduğunu ısrarla savunacaktır. Türkiye, Doğu ile Batı arasındaki barışçıl köprüdür, öyle olmalıdır. Kurucumuzun gösterdiği hedef, milletimizi muasır medeniyetler seviyesine...
(Mikrofon otomatik cihaz tarafından kapatıldı)
BAŞKAN - Devam edin.
ÖZGÜR ÖZEL (Devamla) - Onun işaret ettiği yerde güçlü parlamentolar,
hukukun üstünlüğü, mütevazı liderler, zengin halklar, kişi başına 45, 50, 55 bin dolar millî gelirler var. Birilerinin gözünü diktiği tarafta ise zengin liderler, fakir halklar, kişi başına 4.500 dolar millî gelir var. Bizim rotamız Atatürk'ün koyduğu hedeftir, çağdaş uygarlık seviyesine ulaşmaktır, Avrupa Birliğidir. 32'si Avrupa'da toplam 85 siyasi partinin üyesi olduğu, Başkan Yardımcısı olduğum Sosyalist Enternasyonal, partimizin Avrupa Birliği üyeliği mücadelesine tam desteğini açıklamış, 85 siyasi parti bu konuda imza atmıştır.
Biz, söylediğimiz sözlerin vatandaşa ne etki oluşturduğunu ölçtüğümüz gibi milletin beklentilerini de ölçüyoruz. Bu kürsüye çıkmadan önce halka "Bütçe yapma yetkisi doğrudan sizde olsa bu parayı nereye ayırırdınız?" diye sorduk. Yüzde 37'si tarıma önem vereceğini, yüzde 28,5'u eğitime para harcayacağını, yüzde 19'u sanayiye ve savunma sanayisine, yüzde 14,2'si sağlığa, yüzde 1,7'si çevreye, yüzde 0,3'ü de diyanete para ayıracağını ifade etmiştir. Halkımız tarımı, sağlığı, eğitimi öncelerken, iktidar ise faizi, garanti ödemelerini ve vergi alınmayan zenginleri öncelemektedir.
İşte, önümüze konulan bütçenin rakamları bunlardır: Bütçe geliri 12,8 trilyon, gideri 14,7 trilyon, açık 1,9 trilyon liradır ve Hükûmetin bu bütçeye koyduğu faiz gideri 1,9 trilyon liradır. Uygulanan akıl dışı politikalarla bütçemiz faize rehin verilmiştir.
(Mikrofon otomatik cihaz tarafından kapatıldı)
BAŞKAN - Sayın Özel, toparlayın.
Buyurun.
ÖZGÜR ÖZEL (Devamla) - Vatandaş boşuna mı "En önemli sorunum ekonomi." diyor? Cebindeki 200 lirayla basıldığı ilk gün, tedavüle girdiği ilk gün, 1 Ocak 2009'da 132 dolar alıyordu, bugün en büyük banknotumuz 6 dolar etmiyor. 200 liraya 2009'da 76 litre benzin alıyordu; bugün 5 litre almıyor, 4,5 litre benzin alıyor; 76 litre, 4,5 litre. 200 liraya 2009'da 500 ekmek alırken bugün 20 ekmek alıyor. Bugün PTT'nin sattığı posta pulu 175 lira ancak en büyük banknotla bir tane pul alınabiliyor, paramız pul olmuştur.
Gelelim bütçenin kimin için hazırlandığına. Bu bütçe kimin bütçesi biliyor musunuz: Bu bütçe maaşları asgari ücrete eşitlensin diye 66 milyar lira bulunamayan emeklilerin değil, 701 milyar liralık vergisi silinen zenginlerin bütçesidir.
(Mikrofon otomatik cihaz tarafından kapatıldı)
BAŞKAN - Sayın Başkan, toparlayın lütfen.
ÖZGÜR ÖZEL (Devamla) - Bu bütçe atanamayan öğretmenler için kaynak yaratma bütçesi değil, ihalelere servet transfer etme bütçesidir. Bu bütçe sözde tasarruf yapmak için bir öğün yemek verilmeyen yoksul öğrencilerin değil, lüks araçların yanı sıra artık uçaklardan konvoy yapanların bütçesidir. Bu bütçe açlık sınırının altında maaşla geçinen emeklinin, asgari ücretlinin değil, bugüne kadar kur korumalı mevduattan 1,8 trilyon lira ödenenlerin bütçesidir. Bu bütçe staj ve çıraklık mağdurlarının, emeklilikte adalet isteyenlerin değil, kamuda 3-4 kıyak maaş alanların, yönetim kurulu üyeliği kapanların bütçesidir. Bu bütçe hakkı olan desteği alamadığı için ürünü tarlada kalanların, ürününü yola dökenlerin değil,
adrese teslim ihaleler verilenlerin, teşviklerle semirtilenlerin bütçesidir. Bu bütçe ekmek parası için yerin yüzlerce metre altında ter döken madencilerin değil, "Madencilerin fıtratında ölüm var." diyenlerin bütçesidir. Bu bütçe yoksulluk nedeniyle derme çatma bir barakada yanarak ölen 5 kardeşin değil, "Yoksulluğa isyan etmeyin, sabredin." derken lüks makam arabalarında sıra sıra dizilenlerin bütçesidir. Bu bütçe depremzedelerin bütçesi değil, rezerv alan uygulamasıyla rant peşinde koşanların; tevazunun değil, kibrin ve şatafatın bütçesidir.
Millet 31 Mart seçimlerinde Türkiye'de bir iktidar değişim sürecini başlatmıştır. İktidara düşen, milletin iradesine saygı duymaktır. Saygı duymamanın iktidara da millete de hiçbir faydası yoktur.
(Mikrofon otomatik cihaz tarafından kapatıldı)
ÖZGÜR ÖZEL (Devamla) - Başkanım, bir iki dakikada bitiriyorum, sürmez bile.
Bu yanlış yoldan bir an önce dönülmeli; kayyım atayarak, hukuktan uzaklaşarak, yoksulları ezerek bu ülkeye daha fazla zarar verilmemelidir. Bu ülkenin insanlarına daha fazla yazık edilmemeli, Türkiye hızla hukuka dönmeli, adalet hâkim kılınmalı, Meclisin seçilmiş bir milletvekili daha fazla hapiste tutulmamalı, gençler konuşmalı, itiraz edebilmeli, kayırmacılığa son verilmeli, mülakat derhâl kaldırılmalı, eğitimde bilim esas alınıp her ne kadar ihtiyaç varsa o kadar öğretmen atanmalı, emekli, asgari ücretli, memur artık hakkını almalıdır. Demokratik bir ülkede iktidardan bunları yapması beklenir ama bu iktidar ilk seçimde tecelli edecek milletin kararına
hukuk dışı yöntemlerle direnmeyi amaçlamaktadır. Bu şahsi beka direnişi tarihin hiçbir döneminde başarılı olmamıştır, bundan sonra da olmayacaktır. Bu yüzden artık sandık milletin önüne konulmalıdır, kararı halk vermelidir, kararı millet vermelidir. Bu millete daha fazla zulüm edilmemelidir. Biz bunları yapmaya geliyoruz. Gülmeyen yüzleri güldürmeye, doymayan karınlarını doyurmaya, olmayan adaleti getirmeye, eşitliği getirmeye geliyoruz, bu ülkeyi ayağa kaldırmaya geliyoruz. Yüzyıl önce olduğu gibi yine kurtarmaya, yine halkın iktidarını kurmaya geliyoruz.
Hepinizi saygıyla selamlıyorum. (CHP sıralarından ayakta alkışlar; İYİ Parti ve Saadet Partisi sıralarından alkışlar)
records.englishTranslation:
ON BEHALF OF CHP GROUP ÖZGÜR ÖZEL (Manisa) -
Dear President of the Turkish Grand National Assembly, esteemed Council, esteemed Chairmans, Co-Chairmen, Group Chairmen, Deputy Group Chairmen and esteemed MPs of the political parties, esteemed workers serving in the General Assembly and the Parliament, and our esteemed people watching us on television; I greet you all with love and respect on behalf of the Republican People's Party Group.
As I begin my words, I wish God's mercy to the 6 martyrs who lost their lives in the helicopter disaster in Isparta today, and my condolences to their grieving families and our nation.
Again, I would like to start by expressing my condolences to the families and may God have mercy on our 4 brothers from Giresun Bulancak who lost their lives in the landslide disaster in Artvin Arhavi last weekend and whose bodies were found today.
As I have expressed many times before from this platform, the right to budget is the most important right in the history of humanity and democracy, acquired by paying heavy prices as a result of difficult struggles against monarchies and one-man regimes. This right is a right that imposes on the elected people the heavy responsibility of balancing the right hand that collects taxes and the compassionate left hand that distributes revenues, on the scales of justice and their own conscience. For this reason, the deputies who have received the authority to make a budget from the nation and who are in this hall today carry a responsibility that will never be forgotten towards the citizens whose sovereign rights they represent. Therefore, seeing the budget negotiations held under this roof as just numbers is a matter of throwing up your hands.
Considering it as a routine process to be downloaded and passed will be recorded as a clear abuse of the nation's expectations and the authority it has given to this Parliament.
Today, I stand before you as the Chairman of the Republican People's Party, which founded the Republic of Türkiye with its nation and achieved great development in a short time by preparing the first budgets of this state. Last year, I was at this podium as a newly elected Chairman. That day, unlike today, we were the main opposition party and the 2nd party in the last elections. Now, as the Chairman of the Republican People's Party, which was the 1st party in Türkiye in the March 31 elections by the will of the nation, I carry both the pride and responsibility of being on this podium. (Applause from CHP benches)
Since the 1980 coup passed over all organizations, all political parties and all unions with tanks, the door to success in the political castle was closed to the Republican People's Party. We opened this door with the inspiration we received from our founder and with the three keys he inherited, namely women, youth and science; We unlocked the energy of women and young people by combining the power of science with our party's centuries-old experience.
The Republican People's Party is a state-establishing party. We pride ourselves on the respect of our party and all its members for the state, rushing to the military when the state calls, paying their taxes, and standing in the right place in any situation that may put the state in a difficult situation.

One of our principles is statism, but when those who govern the state personalize the power and destroy the state
If instead of governing with merit and justice, they used it for their own interests and eliminated the distinction between state and party, then it means that someone has pitted the state against the nation, and if the state and the nation come face to face, the nation always wins. Just as when Kenan Evren pointed out a prime minister candidate with a military background, the nation did not elect him, and on the evening of July 15, the nation shielded its chest against those who were Fetullah's soldiers, not Ataturk's, and this is the story of the March 31 elections. History has repeated itself for those who set the state against the nation. (Applause from CHP benches)
In the provinces where Anadolu Agency, which Atatürk founded to give accurate news from the front, and TRT, which continues its life with the tax of 86 million, were turned into monophonic broadcasting organs closed to the opposition, district governors participated in election tours, governors were expected to perform as provincial leaders, and the AK PARTY lost the elections, the governors said, "You have failed." The state and the nation were brought face to face at a time when members of our beloved army were asked to vote in towns and districts where they had never lived and where they would never live. In such an environment, the Republican People's Party stood on the same side with the nation, competed with the state and the nation, and again the nation won. The Turkish alliance, which includes the nation itself, not political parties, and defends unity and solidarity rather than division, fighting and conflict, has won. It is our populist and public administration approach that brings the Turkish alliance together and brings the nation and the state together with our local governments. this
Our citizens, who found our claim and will to carry it into general politics sincere, gave the strongest message that night that what they expected from the country's administration was a developmentalist, solidarity-based, welfare-based administration.
Just as we provide public services with this understanding in our municipalities and local governments and present ourselves to our citizens with budgets that center these services, we claim to have a vision of the same power and to make the budgets of that vision at the central level, but the government has not received the message given by the voters at the ballot box and has not learned a lesson from it. With the budget it brought to this Parliament, the government is again showing to 86 million people that it is standing on the wrong side and insists on standing on the wrong side.
This budget is not developmental, this budget is not solidarity-based, this budget is not welfare-based. It is not fair when collecting taxes, and the heavy tax burden falls on the poor and wage earners. There is no equitable distribution, budget resources are used in favor of the rich circle, not the poor who make up the majority of this society. Politics is about setting priorities; The government admits what its political preference is in the articles of the budget proposal before you.
In my speech evaluating the election results on the evening of March 31, I said, "We stand by our unity and solidarity against all kinds of discrimination; humility, not arrogance, has won." I said. "Our success will not be anyone's defeat. We see these results not as a victory that will make us complacent, but as a credit extended to us by the voters." we said.

of the nation
We tried to read the message on the ballot box correctly. The nation responded to politics by saying, "Stop fighting, solve my problem." Because he said, we struggled to turn abnormal politics into normal.
As we entered the new year this year, as Türkiye's 2nd party, I called our President of the Parliament and the Chairman of the parties represented in the Parliament after us, one by one, according to their number of deputies, and congratulated them on their new year. That day, I did not call Mr. Erdoğan, who did not call me when I was elected Chairman. After the local elections, on April 10, Ramadan Feast, this time, with the responsibility of being Türkiye's first party, I called our 16 Chairman, including Mr. Erdoğan, and congratulated them on their holidays. I visited Mr. Erdoğan at the Headquarters of the Justice and Development Party on May 2, and we hosted him in our party on June 11. At the same time, we hosted the Honorable Chairman and Co-Chairmen of other political parties in our party or visited them in their headquarters and offices in the Parliament with the appointments we requested.
Of course, I also talked about current, hot political issues in every meeting I had, but I persistently brought up the problems of retirees, minimum wage earners, farmers, unassigned teachers, and those waiting for justice, because this was normal, this was what was normal.
"What is normalization?" Let me express it once again to those who ask: Normalization means getting rid of the vicious fights of politics and personal debates and focusing only on the nation's agenda. Normalization is against those who want to polarize the country and consolidate their position by getting caught up in the comfort of abnormal politics.
It is to create a policy that considers the comfort of the nation, but the government has not shown sincerity in this regard in every step it has taken because it sees getting rid of this comfort as costly for itself. It is normal for this Assembly to work overtime to give retirees their rights, but it is not normal to not talk about it, fight and shed blood on the marble of this General Assembly Hall. It is normal to make motions for workers, unappointed teachers, women and children in this Parliament, but it is abnormal to not see or hear them and to raise your hands and reject them. It is normal for Can Atalay, the elected MP of Hatay, to sit here today and represent the people of Hatay who voted for him, but it is not normal to be held in prison like a political prisoner. Tayfun Kahraman's release, Vera's reunion with her father, and the freedom of Gezi detainees are normal because they are required by law.
For all 86 million of us, "What is he doing against us?" "Who benefits politically from this?" Without thinking, we continue to do, demand and fight for what is normal for the whole nation. We no longer allow any artificial agenda or conflict of interests to suppress the voice of the nation, and we will not do so in the future.

(Applause from CHP benches)
With this understanding, right after the local elections, we started to work with all our strength in our municipalities, on the one hand, to support our citizens who were left helpless by the government, and on the other hand, to give a voice to any segment of society that is victimized and seeking rights.
We went to the squares; We held an education rally in Istanbul, a retirement rally in Ankara, a wheat rally in Hayrabolu, a tea rally in Rize, a labor rally in Gebze, a hazelnut rally in Giresun, a peanut rally in Gaziantep, a farmers' rally in Manisa, a right to life rally against terrorism and violence in Beşiktaş, Istanbul, but those in power today did not want to hear, see or talk about these problems. The Justice and Development Party lost the election for the first time in twenty-two years, but "Why did I lose?" Instead of thinking like this and correctly reading the message of the nation at the ballot box, he preferred to insist on abnormal politics.
Today, our nation is faced with a government that puts its own interests above Türkiye's interests and its unjust policies. Since the local elections, artificial agendas have been imposed on both us and the nation for this very purpose. First, an artificial debate was started by calling the "new constitution" to create a fog on the real agenda, and 86 million people were kept busy just to cover the political future of a single person with a constitutional cover. For this reason, even the first 4 articles of the Constitution were targeted and attempts were made to open them up for discussion. With this discussion, an attempt was made to create a smoke screen over the real agenda and real problems of the citizens.
For this reason, the Republican People's Party did not swallow the sugar-coated poison and did not make the nation swallow it. The Republican People's Party has not and will not sit at the Constitutional table with those who do not comply with the current Constitution. (Applause from CHP benches)
When the government could not get results from this, the nation
"Israel will attack us" to divert attention while women, children and babies are being subjected to violence while being crushed under the high cost of living. They chose to start the discussion.
The Republican People's Party requested a closed session in the Parliament to explain what kind of threat Türkiye is facing. The fiction was revealed in the closed session, where nothing was said that the public did not already know, and even the bill proposal, which aimed to impose new taxes on the citizens through this agenda, was withdrawn. Then he said, "There is no Kurdish problem in Türkiye." The government said, "Let Öcalan come, let him speak in the Parliament, let this issue be solved." He chose to start another discussion by saying. "I did it." They entered into a new imposition with this understanding. However, the Republican People's Party, with its historical experience, puts the correct recipe for the solution of the Kurdish problem before Türkiye. This problem must be resolved by democratic and peaceful means. Our suggestion is that a sincere, transparent process based on social consensus should be carried out on the floor of the Parliament representing 86 million people, without excluding any actor.
The Republican People's Party also stated that the consent of the families of martyrs and veterans was not obtained, and their "Yes." He stated from the very first day that he would not be involved in any process that he would not say. (Applause from CHP benches)
Since the government has not been able to achieve the desired results from any of the steps, this time it is greedy for a process that will take away the nation's right to choose and drag Türkiye into a new darkness.

Municipalities that cannot be won at the ballot box are being tried to be occupied through desktop operations.
A FETO-like conspiracy was established against Ahmet Özer, the Mayor of Esenyurt, Türkiye's largest district, with a dawn operation. A secret witness was produced because none of the documents in the investigation, which had no accusations or evidence, and in the illegal search obtained, were of sufficient quality to form the basis of an indictment. A prosecutor who boasts of writing an indictment in a case with 200 defendants in four days has not been able to write an indictment in a case with a single defendant for forty days. Subsequently, a trustee was appointed to our Ovacık Municipality. The funeral ceremony he attended twelve years ago upon the request of the prosecutor was framed as a cover and the sentence was imposed. In both examples there is no justice, no democracy; There are FETO tactics, there are conspiracies, there is fabricated evidence. In addition to our municipalities, trustees were appointed to DEM PARTY municipalities with the same unlawfulness. Today, there is an occupation of 8 municipalities elected by the people on March 31, by a government that succumbed to its political ambitions, did not recognize the election results, and abused state power. In other words, the government is again on the wrong side, pitting the nation against it, but we have not surrendered to evil, and we will not surrender. Just as we have destroyed all the games of the government so far, we will destroy these games again together with the nation. When you oppose the state, the nation has won, and the nation will win again. (Applause from the CHP benches) I must record with pleasure that a historic agreement has been reached for our democracy under the roof of this Assembly on the unlawfulness of trustees. our party
The Republican People's Party, DEM PARTY, İYİ Party, Felicity Party, Future Party, DEVA Party, Democrat Party, Re-Welfare Party, Labor Party and Turkish Workers' Party submitted a joint bill to the Parliament to abolish the trustee regulation. I would like to express that I find it very important that the Nationalist Movement Party also expresses its request for a regulation in this field. Support for the bill or joint work aimed at this purpose will eliminate this unlawfulness and pave the way for Türkiye. It is time to put an end to this shame of democracy.
The most severe attack on our municipalities is undoubtedly the trustees, but it is not limited to this. The government, which has fallen out of favor with the people, has launched an all-out attack against our municipalities in every field. On the election night, Mr. Erdoğan, who lost for the first time in twenty-two years, went to the balcony and said, "We will stand tall, but we will not become steep." Even though he says, he does not stop even for a moment before he becomes erect with this unexpected behavior of the nation; This is political indigestion. Instead of treating this disease, the government prefers to punish voters who did not vote for them and virtually challenge the nation. You fall out of people's hearts, the way to win it back is to work. The door of the nation's heart is open to those who understand, work and try to understand themselves, but if you ignore the nation and resist its decisions, you will fall out of favor, and there is no solution for this. AK PARTY has fallen out of favor with the nation with these actions. Last week, Tayyip Bey asked, "How can the Republican People's Party get so many votes and win so many municipalities?"
he said.

When he said how we won so many municipalities, he sincerely showed that he was in deep questioning, and he is not wrong. Mr. Erdoğan also measures, we also measure. What surprised Mr. Tayyip was not the results of March 31, but the results of October 31. Citizens' average satisfaction with our municipalities has increased to 58 percent after seven months. Because mayors from the Republican People's Party serve well. Just as the Republican People's Party came first in the last elections, it is also Türkiye's first party by a wide margin, both in the polls before Mr. Erdoğan and in our own polls. (Applause from CHP benches) Mr. Erdoğan is actually rebelling against this. Behind this rebellion are the actions that the government could not stop. We have nurseries in 81 provinces, the number of which has increased to 653, with the aim of increasing it to 1,000 in 2025. These nurseries provide services free of charge to the children of martyrs and veterans, and to the children of poor families at 1/10 of the price of the private sector. Those who saw this and wanted to close those nurseries were caught red-handed. We said Hodri challenge. "Shut down the nurseries, otherwise we will come and close them." Those who said, had to deny the articles they sent and take a step back when women, children and families reacted. I say it once again: We will continue to build nurseries. Come on, take a poor man's child out of those nurseries and let's see. (Applause from CHP benches)
Of course, nurseries are not the only ones behind Mr. Erdogan's rebellion. Behind Mr. Erdoğan's rebellion is the city that offers 4-course meals for the price of half soup, the number of which has increased to 76 across the country.
We have restaurants. Behind Mr. Erdoğan's rebellion are our student dormitories, the number of which reaches 70, because the government built enough dormitories for only 13 percent of the students in Türkiye, this figure is 2.6 percent in Istanbul. The responsibility for building a dormitory belongs to the government, but our municipalities have taken responsibility and taken responsibility to prevent our children from being pushed into the arms of sects and communities. Behind Mr. Erdogan's rebellion is "If CHP comes, social aid will be cut." Our municipalities, which increased social aid by 4.8 times against black propaganda, have compassionate left hands. Behind Mr. Erdogan's rebellion are the services of our municipalities, which provide all kinds of support to producers, purchase the products left in the fields and distribute them to poor citizens. Behind Mr. Erdoğan's rebellion is basically the end of an order. Mr. Erdoğan rebels against the end of the order of those who sold Ankara parcel by parcel, those who found plots of land in Istanbul by helicopter and marketed them to Arab sheikhs, and those who betrayed Istanbul. (Applause from CHP benches)
Today's "SGK debts" debate is an attempt to make CHP municipalities pay the bill, with interest, for the food that AK Party municipalities ate for years and did not pay for, all at once. They are being condescended to do something that will cut off the debts of municipal companies at their so-called source. Everyone knows that immediately after these incomes are cut, the Plan and
With a bill to be forwarded to the Budget Commission, companies' interest will be forgiven and their principal debts will be divided into installments.

In order to prevent municipal companies from benefiting from the regulation to be made here, the accumulated high-interest debts of the municipalities - which were also increased during your period - are being tried to be cut off at the source at once. Our nation should know that the money the government means is the sustenance of the children of municipal workers; The money that is meant is the food that the poor eat in city restaurants, the money that is meant is our services in nurseries; The student's scholarship is the social aid of the poor, that is, the money that is meant is the nation's money. Today, we are faced with a mentality that tries to oppress the nation by deducting the nation's money from the nation. On the days when the budget of our government is being discussed, we will discuss together in this hall a large solidarity and developmental budget that will include these from the general budget into the investment plan and spread to every neighborhood, let alone attacking the existing nurseries, dormitories and city restaurants.
Yes, the AK PARTY has won elections for years and is used to winning, but the weakness of the AK PARTY, what it is not used to and does not know, is losing elections. Losing actually means digesting, looking for mistakes in yourself to win the next election; It does not mean tripping your opponent, setting a trap, or sneakily lowering the wheel of a municipal service vehicle at night. What is being done today is to challenge the nation with political indigestion and to stand against the nation again; The nation will never forgive this.
The government is committing all these unlawful acts both to intimidate the opposition and to prevent them from talking about the real agenda of the country, but we
We will stubbornly continue to talk about the real agenda.
We are holding budget discussions at the General Assembly today, but millions of people working for minimum wage are focused on the Minimum Wage Determination Commission meeting that will start tomorrow. The ratio of minimum wage earners to total employees in European Union countries is 9 percent, in Germany this rate is 6 percent because the minimum wage is a wage from which the employee must quickly move away after one year of seniority. However, the rate of workers earning minimum wage in our country was 38 percent ten years ago, and today it has increased to 57 percent. While the minimum wage is a wage that should be quickly avoided with seniority, unfortunately, it has become a monster trying to swallow all workers, and the minimum wage has turned into a basic wage.
On February 1, when the minimum wage, which is 17,002 liras today, first entered the citizens' pockets, the hunger limit was 16,257 liras and the minimum wage was only 745 liras more than the hunger line. Before the election, he said, "We will adjust the minimum wage for inflation 3-4 times a year." Those who said they did not raise a penny in a year. Today, the purchasing power of the minimum wage has decreased by 6 thousand liras compared to the day it was given, falling to 11,004 liras in January. Today, the minimum wage is 3,500 lira below the hunger line. In order for a worker to earn a starvation wage, he must work forty days a month, not thirty days. Eleven months ago, he was buying 42.5 kilos of minced beef with minimum wage, but now he is buying 26 kilos; not done
Due to the increase, 16.5 kilos of minced meat are stolen from the table of minimum wage earners every month. While the minimum wage was 5.5 quarters of gold eleven months ago, today it is 3 quarters of gold. Especially if we go back to the beginning of your government, the minimum wage, which received 7 quarters of gold in 2002, now costs 3 quarters of gold.

If a retiree loses 1 quarter of gold from his pocket once, he is in a position to go back and look for that gold all day long, but at the end of the twenty-two years of your government, not one retiree, but every retiree, has lost not 1 gold but 5.5 gold coins, not once but every month. Something is found where it is lost. Retirees know that they lost what they lost in a ballot box, the ballot box of November 3, 2002, and they will get back what they lost in the first ballot box they find in front of them. (Applause from CHP benches)
On the other hand, the demand for a fair increase in the minimum wage is the common demand of the three major trade union confederations. What DİSK, TÜRK-İŞ, HAK-İŞ say is aimed at preventing further exploitation of hard work. While the actual inflation of the minimum wage has exceeded 80 percent today, that is, the standard expenses of a minimum wage earner have increased by 80 percent compared to a year ago, and even the TÜİK inflation is 47 percent, there are unjust people who say that a 25-30 percent raise for workers is reasonable. They are trying to get the starving minimum wage to get used to these figures, we will not fall for this trick.
The Republican People's Party's minimum wage proposal for only the first half of 2025 is 30 thousand lira. "Our minimum wage offer is 30, we are not below that." (Applause from the CHP benches) And regarding the minimum wage, from 2016 until today, that is, from the period that served as an excuse for this unbalanced, uncontrolled system called the "Presidential government system" to today, "What happened to the minimum wage in the world, what happened today?" If you look at it, from 2016 to 2024, in euro terms, the minimum wage in Greece increased from 693 euros to 969 euros, and it took 41 percent of the time; In Germany, the eight-year minimum wage increase is 42 percent; In Estonia, the minimum wage increase is 91 percent; In Ukraine, which is at war, it is 250 percent, and in Moldova, which has increased the minimum wage the most, it is 474 percent. While the minimum wage in a country with a well-established system like Germany increased by 42 percent in euro terms, the increase in Türkiye is 6.8 percent, and considering how the euro is suppressed, I have to express with shame that in real terms, the minimum wage is the only minimum wage in the world that has decreased in euro terms.
Also, "thank God, we did not let the minimum wage earner be crushed by inflation" for years. I ask those who make propaganda: Which inflation did you not crush? Between 2021 and 2024 only in the last three years
While the gross increase of the minimum wage is 459 percent, the inflation of beef is 738 percent, the inflation of lamb is 719 percent, the inflation of medicine is 669 percent, the inflation of rent is 580 percent and the inflation of mixed food in these three years is 509 percent. In this case, we expect you to find out from TÜİK data which inflation does not oppress the minimum wage earner. What I see is that there is only a ping-pong ball, except for the ping-pong ball, retirees have been crushed by all inflation. (Applause from CHP benches)
Of course, Erdoğan, who was in charge of everything in the transition to the Presidential government system, had an important economic determination: "Interest is the cause, inflation is the result." he said. I ask our Vice President, Mr. Cevdet Yılmaz, who will come to this podium soon to answer our criticisms: Which one do you agree with? "Interest is the cause, inflation is the result.

", and do you agree with Erdoğan, who lowered the interest rates out of spite and brought inflation to this point while the whole world was making reasonable interest increases to combat inflation, or do you believe that Mehmet Şimşek, who was sitting next to you a moment ago and said, "We came, we left these irrational things", has returned to rationality? Is Erdoğan irrational, or is Mehmet Şimşek distorting the facts? It is important for all of us to hear which of these two theses you agree with here.
Thanks to this policy that is contrary to reason and science, we can also increase the Consumer Confidence Index above 90 as the elections approach. At the point of pumping money into the market, this money is paid at 8 percent interest through the Credit Guarantee Fund.
You distributed it to the supporters and the rich in such an extraordinarily selective way, with a selectivity that you boast about. Yachts were bought, cutters were bought, as you all know, this money went to summer houses and all kinds of luxury expenses; That money is currently still paid at 8 percent. On the same days, tradesmen who were in a difficult situation took out a loan from the Tradesman Guarantee Cooperative at 9 percent and said, "Interests have increased, friends," while paying their installments. It was said that the tradesman guarantee loan taken at 9 percent is paid at 25 percent, but the quota taken from the Credit Guarantee Fund at 8 percent remains in the marina and is repaid at 8 percent. This is a great injustice, it is a matter of making choices in terms of politics. You are on the side of the cutters, you are on the side of the rich; We are on the side of the tradesmen and poor citizens. (Applause from CHP benches)
Speaking of "tradesmen", before the elections, they went to the squares and turned to the tradesmen and said, "I know your problem with the number of premium days, which is 9000 working days. I hope we will reduce it to 7200 days first." I would like to remind you once again, on behalf of all tradesmen, of Erdoğan's words.
Now I'm asking you more clearly: The minimum wage should be 30 thousand liras for the first half of 2025, but this cannot be left on the backs of small tradesmen and SMEs who will pay this money to them; We have a bill for this. As you know, if the minimum wage increases to 30 thousand liras, SSI's premium collection will increase by 1 trillion liras. If you introduce an incentive system worth slightly more than 1 quarter of this 1 trillion, that is, between 1 and 10.
With an incentive system of 6 thousand liras per employee for employees and 3 thousand liras for those between 10 and 50, this burden will no longer be a burden on small tradesmen. The minimum wage should be 30 thousand liras for my brother who works in the barbershop, and 24 thousand liras for the master who employs him in the barbershop; We recommend that the minimum wage should be 30 thousand liras for the waiter working in the restaurant and 24 thousand liras for the small tradesmen who run the restaurant, and that this money should not come out of the state's pocket. Our proposal is in the records of the Parliament, entrusted to the valuable members of the Turkish Grand National Assembly. We are in a great struggle, we are in serious preparation, so that those who work in an economy that we will revitalize and develop with investments that will create public benefit will not be forced to earn the minimum wage, will not have difficulty making ends meet, and will not have the minimum wage as the basic wage.
Mr. Erdoğan had declared 2024 as the "Year of Retirees", but it would not have happened; The first blow to the retiree was dealt at the beginning of the year. The minimum pension was increased from 7,500 lira to 10 thousand lira.

While TÜİK's 2023 inflation was 64 percent, retirees were given a 33 percent raise. With this rate, retirees were crushed by inflation at the beginning of the year. Millions of retirees received a salary of 10 thousand lira for six months.
During our contacts abroad, when I said that the lowest pension in Türkiye is 280 euros, foreign leaders and leaders of other countries we served together mispronounced it.
They thought I did it and tried to correct it as "2,800"; I said: No, 280 euros is the lowest pension in Türkiye. My German counterpart asked me, "Can't it be 2,800?" he asks. The same thing happened in a council meeting of the Socialist International: "Pensioners in Türkiye have to live on 280 euros." When it was said, it was expressed by objecting to the simultaneous interpreter. Dear Vice President, the 280 euro pension that you condemned retirees to in the first six months of last year does not have a translation into a foreign language, German or English, none of you have the right to do this. (Applause from CHP benches)
In the second six months, the minimum pension was increased to 12,500 lira, but only a 25 percent increase was made. Today, 4 million retirees receive 12,500 lira; The average salary of 16 million retirees is 15 thousand lira. With this salary, a retired person goes hungry if he pays rent, and if he eats his fill, he stays on the street. In 2002, the lowest pension was 1.5 minimum wage, but now it is 0.7 minimum wage, 70 percent of the minimum wage, so if you did not touch it at all, the pension would be 25,500 lira at the beginning of the year. While 8 quarters of gold could be purchased with the lowest pension in 2002, today only 2.5 quarters of gold can be purchased, and 5.5 quarters of gold are lost.
Now, there are those who are in a new treason plan against our retirees; The minimum pension is between 13,500 and 15,000 lira
They try to say that it should happen, they want it to remain below the hunger line in 2025. They do not see retirees who cannot give pocket money to their grandchildren, who cannot even go to their hometown once a year, who cover their faces while collecting crushed vegetables and fruits after the market is over. As the Republican People's Party, our unquestionable demand is this: A living is a right for retirees, a minimum wage is a must. (Applause from CHP benches)
Unfortunately, our beautiful country is experiencing collapse in every field. Türkiye, one of the 7 countries that are self-sufficient in agriculture, has become dependent on imports today; Even lentils, chickpeas and dried beans are imported for the first time in our history. The number of our farmers has decreased from 2.8 million to 2.3 million in twenty years. While the population increased by 20 million and 500 thousand farmers should have increased proportionally, let alone the increase, 500 thousand farmers were lost, that is, 500 thousand farmers got discouraged and quit. Today, the average age of farmers in Türkiye has increased to 58. Today, 3 out of 4 young farmers say, "If I find a minimum wage job next year, I am thinking of quitting this job." says. Today, 2 out of every 3 farmers in Türkiye have irrevocable debts because the government has not given farmers their rights for years. The projected gross national product for 2025 is 61 trillion. According to the law, 1 percent should be given to farmers, this money is 615 billion lira.

this law
It was passed in 2006 with the joint votes of the Republican People's Party and the AK Party, but in the budget you have brought today, this money is allocated as 135 billion lira, that is, while the law is 1 percent, 2 per thousand is considered valid, and the farmer is given 1 fifth of his rights. When we make a budget under the rule of the Republican People's Party, we will create a law-abiding state and we will definitely give farmers the 1 percent support they are entitled to. We warn: If farmers are ignored, the nation will starve. Prices are cheap for the farmer and expensive for the consumer. While the beans we plucked in the morning in the bean field I visited in Burdur were sold for 8 liras, in the Bursa market I went to in the afternoon, they were sold for 80 liras, and the same bean was 120 liras in Istanbul and 200 liras in Bodrum that day.
While our farmers are bleeding, our breeders are literally dying. A kilo of beef is bought from the breeder for 345 lira and sold in the market for 650 lira. Mr. Erdogan realized that red meat was expensive in Türkiye and found the solution to import animals from South America. This year, 520 thousand head of cattle will be imported. These imports have already been made since 2010, so the manufacturer is disconnected from production and this is mainly where the problem stems from. We complain to our nation about those who turned the Meat and Milk Institution, which was established to support producers, into an import institution. We will restore the Meat and Milk Institution to its real function. The public's responsibility is to ensure citizens' healthy nutrition and access to food, and institutions and rules are needed for this. The government destroyed those institutions, we
We are determined to uphold both institutions and rules.
The government that does not give farmers their due does not have mercy on children either. In our country, approximately 10 million children are in poverty and 2 million children are in deep poverty. The proportion of children who can consume one type of meat once a day is only 12 percent. In Türkiye, 1 in 3 students go to school without breakfast, and 1 in 5 students cannot eat anything at school. You embraced our proposal to provide free meals to students during the elections and gave up after the elections, and here, as members of the People's Alliance, you rejected the bill we made on this issue. The government, which finds resources for every supporter, for some reason cannot find resources to feed our children. You will see with your own eyes that we found that resource in the first budget of the Republican People's Party and Türkiye alliance and that we fed our children. (Applause from CHP benches)
Prices in school canteens present the nutritional problem of our children as one of our real agendas. Children working in the marketplace in Yenibosna, Istanbul: "I'm working for pocket money." they said to me. The price of simit is 20 lira in the canteen, small water is 10 lira, tea is 20 lira, toast is 50 lira; If a student buys water, he or she becomes hungry; if he buys a bagel, he becomes thirsty. Especially if a student buys a cup of tea or a bagel in the morning and supplements it with ayran, coke or water instead of toast at noon, the total money he pays is 100 liras, or 2,500 liras per month. What a father who listens to Mr. Erdoğan and has 3 children will give to his child, eating water and toast?
The total allowance he will give to his child is 7,500 lira. Today, there are credit books in canteens.

When we are in power, we will pass a budget in this Parliament that will provide 3 bowls of hot meals a day to our students.
Mr. Vice President, if you receive an instruction from here, municipalities with Republican People's Party are ready to serve hot meals and free, healthy water to students in all schools in their regions or even nearby. (Applause from CHP benches) We are just waiting for your instructions from here. I would also like to complain to our nation that our municipalities that want to do this are blocked at the school gates.
Now, the government, which cannot create a future for children, cannot give hope to young people. There are home youth expressed by the concept of "NEET" all over the world. These young people are neither in education nor in employment. Europe is very worried about this, Germany is shaken by this problem. The rate of children and young people in Germany who are neither in work nor in education is 6 percent. The rate that Europe is worried about is 9 percent. In Türkiye, this rate is 25 percent, meaning 1 in every 4 young people is neither in education nor in employment. Today, 3 out of every 4 young people say, "I would like to live abroad if I had the opportunity." says; So to speak, 75 percent of our young people have their luggage packed in their minds.
In our parliament, some of our esteemed chairmen describe different survival problems and say: "The developed countries of the world are calculating and dreaming about us." Let them study this
It is our duty to eliminate the survival problem, just as we eliminated it a hundred years ago, and to put those who make plans on the lands of this country to their place. But the real survival problem is not that developed countries dream in these lands, but that three quarters of this country's children dream in developed countries. We undertake and promise to help the youth of this country from all walks of life dream in Anatolia and Thrace during our reign. (Applause from CHP benches)
We are in such an unfair system that the society has become fed up with it, and injustice in taxes is the culmination of this. The rate of indirect taxes in the 2025 budget is 65 percent. Indirect taxes are the most unfair tax in the world, a type of tax that does not discriminate between rich and poor; The manufacturer and the worker or watchman working in that factory go to the dairy and pay the same tax on cheese; Those who drive Türkiye's most expensive jeep and those who drive a tired thirty-year-old tractor pay the same tax on diesel. This rate is 65 percent. 20 percent of taxes are deducted through deductions before they even touch the salary earners. The tax planned to be collected from those who actually earn money is 14.5 percent in this budget, and we have experienced from last year that the actual realization is around 11 percent. 37 of the 44 companies that are the contractors of the projects that the Government boasts about, which absorbs Türkiye's resources, did not pay any taxes last year, and tax-free declarations belong to these 37 companies. forty thieves
He does not put his hand in his pocket, but the state does not take its hand out of the pockets of employees, blue, white and gray collar workers. This tax system is a system that oppresses the poor and favors the rich, and again, a corporate tax exemption of 701 billion liras is introduced in this budget. You say "Resource, resource, resource", but you can find the resource you are looking for where only 1/5 goes to effective incentives and 4/5 remains in someone's pocket.

We will continue to provide the right incentives and develop with the impact analyzes we will make in the budgets we make, and we will shift the wrong ones to the right ones and grow and prosper together.
At the beginning of my speech, I referred to the first budget of the republic and reminded me of the discussions of the first budget of the Republic of Türkiye in 1924. Deputy of Finance Abdülhalik Bey made the following statement from the podium: "We will quickly abolish the tithe tax that oppresses the people." Less than a year later, they kept their promise and abolished the tithe tax, which took 1-tenth of what the producer produced. Today, in the 100th year of this Parliament, which abolished the tithe tax, there is such an unfair tax system that today, we know that white-collar workers earning 12 salaries pay 3 to 4 salaries in taxes. An employee who earns a salary of 33 thousand liras pays 3 net salaries to taxes throughout the year, while a white-collar worker who earns a salary of 66 thousand liras, that is, a salary at the poverty line, pays 4 net salaries to taxes. In the past, white-collar workers said, "This is my salary, but I will get 16 salaries." Today, white-collar workers who earn 12 salaries pay 4 of their salaries to taxes. This Parliament is over
abolished it, but employees are subject to Erdoğan's excessive taxes. Those who used to earn 16 salaries work for 8 salaries and pay 4 salaries to Erdoğan's tithe tax; We rebel against this tax. (Applause from CHP benches)
On the other hand, when we say "resource", those who cannot find resources - it is their duty in the law - have not published the list of tax havens since the day the law was enacted, for exactly eighteen years, since 2006, under Mr. Erdoğan, for a time under Mr. Binali Yıldırım, and for the last seven years, again under Mr. Erdoğan. However, the law is clear, tax havens are clear; Tomorrow morning, if tax havens are published in the Official Gazette, 30 percent tax will be collected from the money going to those heavens. Whoever sends money to Bermuda, Luxembourg, Virgin Islands, Isle of Man, Caymans, Malta, Seychelles is the one you have favored for eighteen years. You don't; We will publish this list in the first three decrees of our government, in the first decree that will be issued after appointing ministers and high-level state officials, and we will tax yours. (Applause from CHP benches)
The government not only does not collect the taxes it is due, but also transfers the taxes it unfairly collects from the poor into someone else's pocket. "Not a penny will come out of the citizens' pockets." Unfortunately, you invested 204 billion lira in this budget for the public-private collaborations, bridges, highways and tunnels you mentioned. “Not a penny comes out of any of this.” In their place, those who say
the winds are blowing. Those in place have allocated 204 billion lira for this year and 678 billion lira for the next three years.
As the Republican People's Party, we are never against these investments, we will do the best, we will do the best, but we will not do it with this model to make someone rich. Aren't you the one who huffs and puffs while bringing the pension of 10 thousand liras to 12,500? Those who find 33 billion liras difficult have allocated 204 billion liras to their supporters who do not pay taxes. On the other hand, when paying them, they are paid in dollars, euros, and their own country's inflation is also affected.

But someone ran the thermal power plant in Çayırhan for twenty years, earned a lot of money, a miracle happened, the hen that had been slaughtered and laid the golden eggs was resurrected and came back to our coop and said, "Let's slaughter it again." you say. You give the coal to the person you give it to, you give the power plant to the person you give it to, you give the purchase guarantee: "Pay TL when paying the money, pay in 6 installments." you say. If you weren't ashamed, you should have made a red bow on it. To whom are you giving Çayırhan, whose property are you giving to whom? (Applause from CHP benches)
Therefore, let me say this: On the first day of our power, we will immediately return to the Turkish lira in these projects, we will look at this place from the perspective of public interest, observing the law - Ms. Selin wrote this part, the educated lady was ashamed, she could not say - we will nationalize, we will nationalize, we will nationalize! (Applause from CHP benches)
What to expect from the administration when the situation is bad in the economy? Of course, he should start saving from himself. You have made a savings circular; According to the circular, vehicles would be limited and many restrictions would come. Let me give an example: Dear Vice President, you wrote a letter for 120 thousand vehicles: "Identify the surplus, do not rent, and sell the rest." saying. A thousand surplus vehicles, friends, a thousand vehicles. Of the 120 thousand vehicles, 119 thousand are needed, a thousand are surplus and before these thousand vehicles are disposed of, 3,544 new vehicle purchases have been included in this budget. So, while you continue with those vehicles, those planes, those ostentations and do not save money, Uncle Mithat, retired from the State Hydraulic Works, Aunt Emel, retired from the bank, my brother Muammer, who earns minimum wage, and my sister Leyla, who manages to make do with her father's salary, will look at the empty refrigerator and collect dented products at the evening market. There is no justice like this! The government makes savings from the poor and workers. They cannot appoint 2,500 disabled teachers. "We saved money." they boast. In any case, the government's concern is not to provide welfare to the people; if that were the case, you would not have transferred public banks, insurance companies, ports and Turkish Airlines to the Wealth Fund, then appointed President Erdoğan to head the Wealth Fund, and then removed the Wealth Fund from the Court of Accounts audit. When we arrive, the Court of Accounts will audit all institutions that have escaped inspection, especially the Wealth Fund.
As the economic crisis deepens, the government declares the country unsafe for everyone.
made it happen. Our children are not safe today. According to research, 86 percent of the society says that children are not safe. Narin, a girl only 8 years old, was found under the stones in a sack in the stream, nineteen days after she disappeared. "If a wolf grabs a sheep on the edge of the Tigris, I am responsible." We come from a public management approach that says; It is a will from Hazrat Omar. We couldn't protect an 8-year-old girl, we couldn't identify her killers, but what the whole village knew was, "We have friends in that village, I can't talk." Prosecutors cannot know or find out what your MP knows. According to research, 64 children lost their lives like Narin in two and a half years.
As you all know, another big threat that children and young people face today is drugs.

According to the official reports of the police, the amount of methamphetamine called "crystal" seized in 2023 increased by 2 thousand percent compared to 2019. Unfortunately, drugs have reached the frontiers of schools. Mothers and fathers say to every MP they see, whether in power or opposition, and every politician they reach, "Save our children from this trouble, keep them away." he cries. I invite the two ministers, the successor and the predecessor, who could not attract each other from time to time and could not share their love for Mr. Erdoğan, to share the responsibility for the amount of drugs that increased by 2 thousand percent in four years. (Applause from the CHP ranks) The reasons for this increase in drug use should be investigated, but in most of the studies, the astronomical and unnamed alcohol levels
I would also like to draw attention to the findings that the high level of hostile lifestyle tax is effective.
Women, like children, are not safe. Unfortunately, according to the OECD, we rank first in violence against women in Europe. Almost 1 in 4 women are exposed to violence. 70 percent of women say they cannot walk safely on the streets at night.
This government could not keep its promises to retirees, laborers, this government to miners, this government to tradesmen and farmers. Two days before the election, he told the earthquake victims: "We will build 650 thousand houses in a year. At the end of a year, you will all have your homes." and when two years are up, only 25 percent, and if the issue is Hatay, only 12 percent. 3 out of 4 earthquake victims are in shelters, 3 out of 4 earthquake victims are in tents or abroad in Türkiye. In Hatay, the rate of those who have found their homes is only 12 percent. No promise has been kept, but only one promise has been kept and continues to be kept; Only the promise you made to HUDA PAR not to return to the Istanbul Convention is persistently kept. During the first week of power of the Republican People's Party, the Istanbul Convention will be enacted again in the Turkish Grand National Assembly. (Applause from CHP benches)
Babies are not safe in this country either. At least 12 of our newborns became victims of neglect, lack of control, and greed for money. We are talking about negligence that was known nineteen months ago, but no precautions were taken while babies continued to die. Today, not even a public official's statement is taken, it's like a theater is being played in the courts.
The system knows: "If I give the yellow ox, the end will come to us." The system that kills newborns today, causes the death of newborns we do not know about, or causes the relatives and elders of any of us to perish in intensive care units is the system of this government that commercializes and uncontrolles health. In this system, private hospitals obtain licenses and licenses like transfers. Can you believe it? They just rent out the neonatal unit to anyone they want, and those who want to get that rent form gangs, quarrel among themselves, rob the state, and kill babies. I know that this lack of conscience and control that commercializes health hurts all of your hearts. You keep silent, you keep silent, but we all know the real responsible people; Leave the yellow ox, hold on to your conscience. (Applause from CHP benches)
Foreign policy is one of Türkiye's main issues that we need to focus on seriously.

The founder of our country and our party, Gazi Mustafa Kemal Atatürk, is known all over the world as "Peace at home, peace in the world." It's time to remember your promise. We know what kind of legacy Atatürk left us in foreign policy; "Peace at home, peace in the world." He left his vision, he left good relations with neighbors. In the following years, both the Republican People's Party governments and many other governments acted in accordance with this vision; He stayed away from the neighbors' internal debates, did not deal with non-state elements in the neighbor, and defended the neighbor's territorial integrity. However, AK PARTY governments did the exact opposite;
Institutions in foreign policy were excluded, this Parliament was excluded, and most importantly, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs was excluded with its staff, experience and tradition. During the Cyprus Peace Operation, the then Deputy Prime Minister Erbakan gave the first information about the operation in this hall in a closed session on July 18, and the then Prime Minister Bülent Ecevit gave the first information about the operation on July 20. However, the Parliament was completely ignored in the face of all the events in Syria.
The adventurous approach in Syria has created a major security threat to the lives of our citizens since 2011 and has left us facing a major migration problem. We cannot ignore the cost Türkiye pays; We cannot act as if nothing had happened in thirteen years, as if our people had not paid the price for all the mistakes, as if babies Aylan had not died, or as if they had not drowned in the Aegean. First of all, we must learn from past mistakes and quickly move away from the adventurous foreign policy approach.
Assad, who has been ruling our neighbor Syria with authoritarianism for years, was overthrown yesterday, just like in Iraq and Libya.
